message:
fix: paint full window bg, drop status bar padding, fix input bg and viewport jump

Root causes fixed:

1. initTheme() was called after newModel(), so styles were zero-value when
   the textarea was created. The prompt was rendered with an empty style,
   and applyInputTheme had nothing to apply. Fixed: initTheme() now runs
   before newModel() in main.go, and newModel() calls applyInputTheme()
   directly.

2. viewportBg and inputBg were hard-coded ANSI-256 colours (233 dark,
   231 light). If theme detection returns the wrong mode the entire
   viewport and input get painted the wrong solid colour. Fixed: both are
   now empty string (transparent) — only foreground colours are forced;
   backgrounds inherit the terminal's natural colour. Status bar and
   user-message blocks still paint explicit bg since they intentionally
   differ from the terminal bg.

3. SetWidth(m.width - 2) left a 2-char gap. textarea.SetWidth already
   subtracts the prompt width internally. Fixed: SetWidth(m.width).

4. Textarea did not auto-grow. bubbles textarea has no auto-grow; height
   is fixed. Fixed: after each key event, compute wantHeight from
   LineCount(), clamp to [1, MaxHeight], and call SetHeight() + resize
   viewport if it changed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -46,8 +46,8 @@ type colorTheme struct {
 var darkTheme = colorTheme{
 	statusBarBg: lipgloss.Color("235"),
 	statusBarFg: lipgloss.Color("252"),
-	viewportBg:  lipgloss.Color("233"),
-	inputBg:     lipgloss.Color("233"),
+	viewportBg:  lipgloss.Color(""),
+	inputBg:     lipgloss.Color(""),
 
 	connected:    lipgloss.Color("42"),
 	disconnected: lipgloss.Color("196"),
@@ -77,8 +77,8 @@ var darkTheme = colorTheme{
 var lightTheme = colorTheme{
 	statusBarBg: lipgloss.Color("254"),
 	statusBarFg: lipgloss.Color("236"),
-	viewportBg:  lipgloss.Color("231"),
-	inputBg:     lipgloss.Color("231"),
+	viewportBg:  lipgloss.Color(""),
+	inputBg:     lipgloss.Color(""),
 
 	connected:    lipgloss.Color("28"),
 	disconnected: lipgloss.Color("160"),
